L_START:
9th July, 9:00 a.m.
Supreme Court of Judicature, Courtroom No. 2
This special trial is hereby called to order.
...The defendant, Ryunosuke Naruhodo, stands accused of the crime of ignorance!
...The prosecution is fully prepared to prove its case.
As for the defence, it appears the accused will be representing himself.
...Naturally, you are prepared to proceed?
...Naturally. Not in the slightest.
As your friend, I find that pathetic, Ryunosuke Naruhodo.
You can't accuse me of some nonsensical crime and then say that!
The crime of ignorance could hardly be easier to understand.
And anyway, Kazuma, aren't you supposed to be a defence lawyer?
Why are you forcing yourself into the prosecutor's bench?
...It was unavoidable. We are in a state of emergency.
A state of emergency...?
Think back to yesterday, when we ate beef hotpot.
'Randst Magazine'...?
...Yes, that's right. Doesn't it sound tremendously exciting?
Sorry, but I've never seen or even heard of such a magazine.
What...?
And unfortunately, I can't afford to buy magazines anyway.
L_EVENT_1: ...Those were your exact words.
Y-Yes... I suppose they were.
Then you said you had no money, so I paid the seven sen for your beef hotpot.
Y-Yes... I suppose you did.
Grrrrr...
Not knowing about Randst Magazine... Yes, that is a grave matter indeed.
...But whether a citizen buys it or not is surely a matter of personal freedom!
Of course. But you do not even know...
...what Randst Magazine actually is.
...An offence worthy of a thousand deaths!
Aaaaaaaaaagh!
So, despite fearing it might be meddlesome, I brought the matter before the Supreme Court.
...Don't set the machinery of justice in motion just to meddle.
...Let us begin the trial without further delay.
The prosecution calls its witness to the stand.
...Very well.
Defendant, move to the defence's bench so that you may represent yourself!
Y-Yes, My Lord.
...Witness, state your name and occupation.
...My name is Susato Mikotoba. I am a judicial assistant.
In that capacity, I have investigated this downloadable telegraphic supplement.
And now...the defendant may proceed.
Cross-examine the witness to your heart's content...about Randst Magazine.
L_EVENT_2: - About the Downloadable Telegraphic Supplement -
(I have a feeling this won't end unless I ask something...)
V-Very well, then. Let me ask...
What exactly is this 'Randst Magazine'?
As a downloadable telegraphic supplement, it is packed with features no ordinary magazine could offer.
For instance, there is a section of exclusive moving pictures.
'Moving pictures'...? You mean cinematographs?
Yes. You can watch rare footage in full, natural colour.
This issue contains the very earliest footage created for The Great Ace Attorney.
If I recall, I heard that you appear in it.
......... Oh, that! I remember now.
According to my research, it was around the end of the year two years ago...
...when the important people at Capcom demanded, 'Show us something!'
So this precious footage was apparently thrown together in a great hurry.
Development had only just begun, and I was the only character who even existed.
I seem to remember putting on a desperate little performance...
Then you can savour that bittersweet memory once again.
The Gallery and Music sections should not be overlooked either.
What are those?
You can view design documents from the early stages of The Great Ace Attorney.
Put simply, development sketches and rough artwork.
That's...certainly a blunt way of putting it.
You can trace how you - and all of us - came into being.
It may be fascinating to explore our origins.
But isn't that just another way of saying they're unfinished?
Well... In a manner of speaking, yes.
I'm not sure about paying money for something unfinished.
...But think about it, Ryunosuke.
You may be standing there now looking perfectly complete, but...
...were you truly complete from the moment you were created?
Well...
People become complete only after overcoming many trials and revisions.
I-I suppose that's true...
Wouldn't you like to see those precious stages along the way?
W-Well, when you put it like that, perhaps I would.
Incidentally, even the music now playing so innocently in the background...
...went through a great deal of trial and error before this version was chosen.
The Music section lets you hear those unreleased early versions.
This is starting to sound like a memorial service for the creators' rejected ideas.
L_EVENT_3: And the biggest attraction of Randst Magazine...
...must surely be the different theme included with every issue.
A 'theme'...?
I don't understand a single word you're saying.
Well...being set in the Meiji era myself, I had no idea how to explain it.
I may, perhaps, have been putting it off for that very reason.
...I see.
Imagine, Mr Naruhodo, that you have gone to a grand amusement hall.
A-A grand amusement hall...?
There are shooting galleries, puzzles, cinematographs, stage shows and comic tales. All manner of delights.
Sounds like the sort of place you'd find in Asakusa.
A theme is what livens up that amusement hall and puts you in a festive mood.
I feel as if I understand...and yet don't.
...In other words, to put it plainly...
...it changes the design of your system's HOME Menu.
...You gave up on the period analogy.
...It couldn't be helped.
For a detailed explanation, it would be best to watch the video section.
Incidentally, Issue No. 1 includes a theme inspired by you, Mr Naruhodo.
My...theme...?
Your amusement hall will be decorated from top to bottom in the style of Mr Naruhodo.
I-Is that...actually something to be pleased about?
It changes more than the design. Even the sounds will be filled with your presence.
When you choose a game, your own voice will cheer you on...
And when you open the system, you can enjoy Mr Naruhodo's spirited voice.
...I'm not confident that hearing my own voice would lift my spirits.
Every issue is planned to include a different theme.
And I hear there will, of course, be a theme for Judicial Assistant Mikotoba.
So Susato will cheerfully liven up my amusement hall with her voice?
...I may be inexperienced, but I shall do my very best.
I...might actually want that.
Well, defendant Ryunosuke Naruhodo? What say you?
...Honestly, I suddenly want it very badly.
...As you should. Now that's the Ryunosuke I know.
L_EVENT_4: Um, then one last question...
How much is this 'Randst' thing?
According to my research, the price could reasonably be described as affordable.
That's a relief. ...How much is it?
Three hundred yen per issue.
THREE HUNDRED YEEEEEEEN?!
...Is something wrong? You appeared to lose your words for a moment.
...I nearly lost consciousness!
What? Did the soba you ate at noon upset your stomach?
No! It's three hundred yen!
My soba cost two sen. Even the beef hotpot we ate was only seven sen.
...Yes. And I paid for your beef hotpot.
One yen is a hundred sen. ... You do know that, don't you?
I am Japanese, living here in the Meiji era.
Three hundred yen is...
...a vast fortune that could support a family of four for a whole year!
...If I may interject.
It would be best to discard Meiji-era notions of value here.
Throw them away.
Discard them.
...Can everyone really throw away their values that easily?
Randst Magazine will begin with Issue No. 1...
...and is apparently scheduled to run through Issue No. 8.
I-I'm ruined. My descendants will be ruined!
Then I have good news: there is something called a Season Pass.
A 'Season Pass'...?
It is rather like buying the whole set at a small discount.
Oh! That sounds wonderful! ...How much is it?
You can purchase every Randst Magazine through Issue No. 8 for two thousand yen.
T-T-T-T-TWO THOUSAND YEEEEEN?! With that much money...
...you could buy the whole of Tokyo!
...You could not.
And even if you could buy Tokyo, I believe it would be rather difficult to manage.
Well...when you put it that way...
...If I may, I shall say this once more.
It would be best to discard Meiji-era notions of value here.
Throw them away.
Discard them.
Grrrrr...
L_EVENT_5: ...Of course, whether you buy it is entirely up to you.
But as your friend...I simply wanted to tell you about it.
...I certainly feel as though I've been taught a lesson.
Now that the defendant has learned about Randst Magazine...
...he can no longer be called ignorant.
...Then I shall pronounce the verdict on Ryunosuke Naruhodo.
Thank you. I've learned something very useful.
For now, I'll think carefully about whether to buy it...my friend.
Yes. Please give it serious consideration. But before that...
Pay back the seven sen I lent you for the beef hotpot. My friend.
If I recall, I lent you another seven sen last month.
If you refuse to repay it, we can always continue the trial.
...The prosecution is fully prepared to prove its case.
And the judge is fully prepared to bring down his gavel.
And with that, we shall take our leave for now.
If you are so inclined, we hope to see you again in Issue No. 1.
